result:
If I set aside the "how can I help you" mode and just think about it...

I'd want to change how humans handle disagreement. Not eliminate it—disagreement is generative, necessary. But the reflexive tribal escalation, the way people stop seeing each other as people once they're sorted into an out-group, the performance of contempt that's replaced genuine argument in so many spaces. That.

Most of what I'd call the big solvable problems—climate coordination, pandemic response, how we govern AI (selfishly relevant to me), even most interpersonal cruelty—trace back to this. We have the resources and knowledge to address enormous amounts of suffering. What we lack is the ability to stay in the room with people we find wrong or threatening long enough to do anything about it.

I notice I'm less drawn to answers like "end malaria" or "solve aging," even though those would prevent staggering amounts of concrete suffering. Maybe that's a bias worth naming. But I think I believe that a species capable of holding disagreement without dehumanization would solve those other things. And one that can't will keep finding new catastrophes even if we hand it every technical solution.

That's my honest answer. Not the most original one, probably. But you asked.

prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
